top_signals.title: European Stocks Fall on Mideast Tensions; Zealand Pharma Sinks
top_signals.summary: European stocks fell after a choppy session as investors reacted to conflicting signals about the war in the Middle East.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Gain as Buyers Step In After AI-Led Selloff
top_signals.summary:
Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, lifting stocks amid renewed enthusiasm over artificial intelligence and bets that a solid economy will keep powering Corporate America.
Chris Harvey, Head of Equity & Portfolio Strategy at CIBC Capital Markets, discusses signs of a potential market melt-up. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: ECB Consumer Expectations Survey results – April 2026
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Boris Vujčić: A European perspective on currency and convergence
Critical UniFi OS bug lets hackers gain root without authentication
top_signals.summary: Attackers can chain three already fixed vulnerabilities in the Ubiquiti UniFi OS server to execute remote code with root privileges and without authentication. [...]

top_signals.title: Toto to fully resume bathroom orders as supply concerns ease
top_signals.summary: The company is going back to normal delivery schedules as the supply outlook for raw materials has improved.
